name: nbQA (Code Quality Tools for Jupyter Notebooks)

performance.notes: Depends on underlying tool being run

agent_readiness.notes: Run code quality tools on Jupyter notebooks. nbqa ruff notebook.ipynb. nbqa black notebook.ipynb. nbqa isort notebook.ipynb. nbqa mypy notebook.ipynb. nbqa flake8 notebook.ipynb. nbqa pylint notebook.ipynb. nbqa ruff --fix notebook.ipynb. Works with any tool that takes .py files. Extracts code cells to .py, runs tool, maps errors back to notebook. pre-commit integration: - repo: https://github.com/nbQA-dev/nbQA hooks: - id: nbqa-ruff - id: nbqa-black. CI: nbqa ruff . → lint all notebooks. Respects .ruff.toml, pyproject.toml tool config. nbqa --version. nbqa [tool] [notebook] [args]. Output preserves notebook cell line numbers.

gotchas: Line numbers in output correspond to extracted .py not notebook cells
Magic commands (%, %%) and shell commands (!) not linted
Auto-fix may change code behavior in cell-by-cell execution context
Markdown cells ignored — only code cells processed
Some fixers (isort) may reorder imports across cell boundaries
best_when: Applying ruff, black, mypy, or other tools to Jupyter notebooks
Pre-commit hooks ensuring notebook code quality
CI/CD linting of notebooks alongside Python files
Teams using notebooks who want consistent code quality standards
avoid_when: When Ruff's built-in Jupyter notebook support covers the need (Ruff 0.2+)
Simple notebooks where cell-by-cell context makes linting unhelpful
summary: nbQA runs code quality tools on Jupyter notebooks. nbqa ruff notebook.ipynb; nbqa black notebook.ipynb; nbqa mypy notebook.ipynb. Extracts code cells, runs tool, maps errors back. Pre-commit integration. CI-friendly. MIT by nbQA-dev. Magic commands not linted. Ruff 0.2+ has built-in Jupyter support that may replace some nbQA use cases. Best for applying any Python tool to notebooks.
